name: Systemic Lupus Erythematosus
summary: Systemic lupus erythematosus is a multisystem autoimmune disease with polygenic architecture and heterogeneous clinical presentation. GENARCH provides educational population-level summaries only.
adolescent_relevance: SLE can present in adolescence/young adulthood; GENARCH avoids individualized risk or clinical advice.

genetic_architecture.prs_notes: PRS discussed as population-level genetic liability; GENARCH does not provide individual predictions.

exposure_modifiers.mechanism_hypothesis: UV exposure is discussed as a flare trigger in clinical and observational contexts; evidence varies.
Infections can influence immune activation; pathogen-specific evidence is complex.
Smoking is studied in autoimmune outcomes; evidence varies by cohort.

population_equity.gwas_ancestry_breakdown: SLE genetics studies include multiple ancestries but sample sizes vary across groups.
population_equity.transferability_notes: PRS and locus mapping can vary across ancesries due to LD and allele frequency differences.
population_equity.data_gaps: Exposure measurement and outcome definitions differ across cohorts.

references.title: Ultraviolet radiation and autoimmune disease mechanisms: overview (review)
Infection exposure and immune-mediated disease mechanisms: overview (review)
The Health Consequences of Smoking—50 Years of Progress
references.journal: U.S. Department of Health and Human Services
